query: CDC NoroSTAT historical seasonal norovirus outbreak counts by week, typical late-season weekly increment patterns May June July
answer:
## CDC NoroSTAT Historical Seasonal Norovirus Outbreak Counts: Late-Season Weekly Patterns (May–July)

### System Overview

**NoroSTAT** (Norovirus Sentinel Testing and Tracking) is a CDC collaborative surveillance network of **14 state health departments** (CO, MA, MI, MN, NE, NH, NM, NC, OH, OR, SC, TN, VA, WI), established in August 2012. States report suspected and confirmed norovirus outbreaks through NORS within 7 business days. The **seasonal year runs August 1 through July 31**. The historical reference range spans **2012–2013 through 2024–2025** (13 completed seasons).

---

### Current Season Snapshot (2025–2026)

- **Through May 7, 2026**: **1,194 cumulative outbreaks** reported
- **Same period 2024–2025**: **2,534 outbreaks** (an exceptional season driven by the novel GII.17[P17] strain)
- The 2025–2026 season is **within the historical interquartile range (middle 50%)** of prior seasons

---

### Complete Late-Season Weekly Data (May, June, July) — Historical IQR and Range

From the CDC NoroSTAT data table (data page last updated May 20, 2026):

| Week of Onset | Min (2012–25) | 25th Pctile | 75th Pctile | Max (2012–25) | 2025–26 |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **1-May** | 2 | 17 | 29 | 40 | 19 |
| **8-May** | 0 | 18 | 26 | 27 | — |
| **15-May** | 0 | 12 | 19 | 31 | — |
| **22-May** | 0 | 8 | 11 | 17 | — |
| **29-May** | 0 | 7 | 12 | 13 | — |
| **5-Jun** | 0 | 7 | 9 | 13 | — |
| **12-Jun** | 2 | 7 | 9 | 13 | — |
| **19-Jun** | 1 | 6 | 11 | 14 | — |
| **26-Jun** | 1 | 4 | 7 | 11 | — |
| **3-Jul** | 1 | 4 | 7 | 11 | — |
| **10-Jul** | 1 | 7 | 9 | 14 | — |
| **17-Jul** | 0 | 4 | 9 | 11 | — |
| **24-Jul** | 2 | 5 | 7 | 11 | — |

---

### Week-over-Week Increment Patterns

#### May: Rapid Decline from Spring Shoulder
The typical (IQR midpoint) weekly count drops roughly **50–60% over 4 weeks**:

| Transition | 25th %ile Δ | 75th %ile Δ | Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| 24-Apr → 1-May | 21→17 (−4) | 27→29 (+2) | Slight decline begins |
| 1-May → 8-May | 17→18 (+1) | 29→26 (−3) | Upper range contracting |
| 8-May → 15-May | 18→12 (**−6**) | 26→19 (**−7**) | **Sharpest single-week drop** |
| 15-May → 22-May | 12→8 (−4) | 19→11 (−8) | Continued steep decline |
| 22-May → 29-May | 8→7 (−1) | 11→12 (+1) | Flattening out |

**Key insight**: The steepest weekly decline occurs in the **second and third weeks of May**, when typical counts fall from ~18–26/week to ~8–11/week.

#### June: Low Plateau
| Transition | 25th %ile Δ | 75th %ile Δ | Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| 29-May → 5-Jun | 7→7 (0) | 12→9 (−3) | Entering trough |
| 5-Jun → 12-Jun | 7→7 (0) | 9→9 (0) | **Flat plateau** |
| 12-Jun → 19-Jun | 7→6 (−1) | 9→11 (+2) | Essentially stable |
| 19-Jun → 26-Jun | 6→4 (−2) | 11→7 (−4) | Late-June dip |

**Key insight**: June is characterized by a **stable low plateau** at ~7–9 outbreaks/week (IQR) in early-mid June, declining slightly to ~4–7/week by month's end. The historical maximum never exceeds 14 in any June week.

#### July: Seasonal Trough / Inter-Season Low
| Transition | 25th %ile Δ | 75th %ile Δ | Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| 26-Jun → 3-Jul | 4→4 (0) | 7→7 (0) | Flat at trough |
| 3-Jul → 10-Jul | 4→7 (+3) | 7→9 (+2) | Minor mid-July bump |
| 10-Jul → 17-Jul | 7→4 (−3) | 9→9 (0) | Bump recedes |
| 17-Jul → 24-Jul | 4→5 (+1) | 9→7 (−2) | Flat at trough |

**Key insight**: July activity is essentially **flat at the seasonal trough** with ~4–9 outbreaks/week. A slight bump around July 10 is within noise. This is the lowest sustained activity of the year.

---

### Cumulative Late-Season Additions

Over the full **May 1 – July 31** period (~13 weeks), a typical season adds approximately **100–170 outbreaks** to the cumulative total, compared to the **900–1,400 outbreaks** already accumulated by end of April. In proportional terms, the May–July tail adds roughly **8–12%** of the full season total.

| Period | Typical Weekly (IQR) | ~Cumulative Addition |
|---|---|---|
| May (5 weeks) | 7–29/wk declining | 55–95 outbreaks |
| June (4 weeks) | 4–11/wk | 24–36 outbreaks |
| July (5 weeks) | 4–9/wk | 25–40 outbreaks |
| **May–Jul total** | — | **~100–170 outbreaks** |

---

### Broader Seasonal Pattern Context

1. **Trough**: Late July – September (~4–12/week)
2. **Ramp-up**: October – November (exponential growth ~10→40/week)
3. **Peak**: Late November – early March (maximum single-week historical: **205 outbreaks**, week of Jan 23)
4. **Decline**: March – April (~50/week→~25/week)
5. **Late-season tail**: May (rapid decline ~20→~10/week)
6. **Summer trough**: June – July (flat at ~4–9/week)

---

### Genotype Context (2025–2026 vs. 2024–2025)

The anomalously high 2024–2025 season was driven by **GII.17[P17]** (74% of genotyped outbreaks, 306/411). The 2025–2026 season has reverted to **GII.4 Sydney[P16] dominance** (52% of 102 genotyped outbreaks), consistent with a return to typical outbreak volumes.

key_facts: NoroSTAT is a CDC collaborative network of 14 state health departments reporting norovirus outbreaks; the seasonal year runs August 1 through July 31, with historical reference spanning 2012-2013 through 2024-2025 (13 seasons).
As of May 7, 2026, the 2025-2026 season has 1,194 cumulative outbreaks, within the historical IQR; the 2024-2025 season had 2,534 at the same point (an outlier driven by GII.17[P17]).
In early May, typical weekly outbreak counts (IQR) are 17-29 per week; by late May they decline sharply to 7-12 per week — a roughly 50-60% decline over 4 weeks.
The steepest single-week decline occurs between the weeks of May 8 and May 22, when the 75th percentile drops from 26 to 11 outbreaks.
June activity stabilizes at a low plateau of approximately 7-9 outbreaks/week (IQR) in early-mid June, declining slightly to 4-7/week by late June. The historical maximum in any June week is 14.
July represents the seasonal trough at 4-9 outbreaks/week (IQR) with essentially no trend — flat activity. A minor bump around July 10 (25th pctile rises to 7) is within noise.
Over the full May-July period (~13 weeks), a typical season adds approximately 100-170 cumulative outbreaks, representing roughly 8-12% of the full season total.
The historical minimum for any single week from May through July is 0 outbreaks (occurs in multiple weeks); the historical maximum is 40 (week of May 1).
The 2025-2026 season is dominated by GII.4 Sydney[P16] (52% of genotyped outbreaks), returning to typical patterns after the unusual GII.17[P17]-dominant 2024-2025 season.
Average annual U.S. norovirus outbreaks reported nationwide is approximately 2,500; outbreaks are most common November through April.
